tldr: On the 215.1 km queen stage, Benoît Cosnefroy (UAE Team Emirates - XRG) won the uphill sprint from a reduced group and took the overall lead from Julius Johansen, who was distanced on the climbs. Noa Isidore was second on the stage.

narrative.headline: Cosnefroy wins the queen stage and seizes yellow
narrative.body:
The 215.1 km Stage 2 from Aron to Pré-en-Pail-Saint-Samson was the hardest day of the race and the one that decided it. An early six-man breakaway controlled the first half before Lidl - Trek lifted the tempo and the peloton fractured on the repeated late climbs; the move was reeled in with roughly 66 km to go.

The finale became a war of attrition: Valentin Retailleau, then Marco Brenner and Louis Barré, and finally Aubin Sparfel inside the last 10 km all tried to go clear, but Sparfel was caught with about 5 km left. From the reduced front group Benoît Cosnefroy timed the uphill sprint perfectly, winning ahead of Decathlon's Noa Isidore and EF's Vincenzo Albanese.

The stakes were the overall: leader Julius Johansen lost contact on the climbs, and Cosnefroy's win — plus the splits behind him — handed UAE the leader's jersey with one flat stage to come.

gcImpact.headline: Cosnefroy takes yellow; Gruel and Isidore close behind
gcImpact.body: Cosnefroy moved into the overall lead and would carry it to the finish. Behind him the GC tightened around the day's survivors: Thibaud Gruel into second at +7, Noa Isidore third at +12, Mads Pedersen fourth at +13 and Clément Izquierdo fifth at +15. Prologue winner Johansen dropped out of contention after being distanced on the climbs.

riderPerformances.name: Mads Pedersen
riderPerformances.narrative: Lidl - Trek lit the fuse on this stage, raising the tempo that brought back the break and split the field on the climbs — a move that ultimately handed Cosnefroy the platform to win. Pedersen rode through safely in the reduced group and sat fourth overall at +13 afterwards, keeping his options open for the flat Stage 3 finale.

keyMoments.what: Lidl - Trek lifted the pace to catch the break and shatter the peloton on the late climbs

sources.title: Boucles de la Mayenne 2026 Stage 2 result
